result.markdown:
--- title: "West Texas" subtitle: "Regional economy — Sonora, Eldorado, Ozona, and Junction vs. Texas and the US" --- A comparison of the West Texas regional economy to the state of Texas and the United States, focusing on four small towns in the Edwards Plateau and Permian Basin fringe: - **Sonora** (Sutton County, pop. ~3,000) - **Eldorado** (Schleicher County, pop. ~2,800) - **Ozona** (Crockett County, pop. ~3,400) - **Junction** (Kimble County, pop. ~4,400) These communities share an economy shaped by ranching, oil and gas services, and highway commerce. County-level data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics and Bureau of Economic Analysis allows direct comparison to state and national benchmarks. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
US Unemployment
4.3%
Latest month, BLS

Avg of 4 counties
``` ::: ::: ## Sections - **[Unemployment →](unemployment.qmd)** — Monthly unemployment rates for each county vs. Texas and the US, from 2005 to present. - **[Per-Capita Income →](income.qmd)** — Annual per-capita personal income by county, with comparisons to state and national averages. - **[Economic Output →](gdp.qmd)** — Annual GDP for Texas and the US, with county-level output where available from the BEA. - **[Methodology →](about.qmd)** — Data sources, FIPS codes, population context, and caveats about small-county volatility. ## Data sources | Source | What | License | Refresh | |---|---|---|---| | [BLS LAUS](https://www.bls.gov/lau/) | Monthly county unemployment rates | Public domain (US gov) | Monthly | | [BEA Regional](https://www.bea.gov/data/by-place-county-metro-local) | Annual county GDP and per-capita income | Public domain (US gov) | Annual (~6 mo lag) | | [FRED](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/) | US unemployment (UNRATE), Texas unemployment (TXUR), Texas real GSP (TXRGSP) | Public domain | Daily / monthly |
